original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for SNMP?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for Certificates?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what issue was resolved in Internal srvcs: pspo Bug 267398?
original_answer: Internal srvcs: pspo (Bug ID 267398): Symptom: VXLAN tunnels go down after removing interfaces with IPv6 address that are the same as the VXLAN VTEP IP addresses. Scenario: In an EVPN-VXLAN deployment with an IPv6 tunnel, if any interface (irrespective of the VRF) that has same IP address as the tunnel source IP, it goes down, and then the tunnel interface is brought down. Workaround: Unconfigure loopback and VXLAN and re-configure them.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what issue was resolved in WebUI Bug 279019?
original_answer: WebUI (Bug ID 279019): Symptom: After uploading an invalid PEM certificate, the hpe-restd process becomes unstable, the WebUI temporarily stops responding, and all REST API calls from the WebUI fail. Scenario: If a user uploads a corrupt PEM certificate file using WebUI certificate management window, selecting the Upload button in the WebUI causes the WebUI to stop working completely. To recover from this state, restart hpe-restd from the bash prompt in the command-line interface or restart the switch. Workaround: Use the CLI to upload certificates.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what issue was resolved in VSX Bug 278958?
original_answer: VSX (Bug ID 278958): Symptom: MCLAG interfaces unexpectedly shut down on both VSX members by the VSX process. Scenario: During a VSX split condition, the secondary VSX member might shut down MCLAG interfaces when the VSX primary member connects and reestablishes the ISL connection, but before the primary brings up its MCLAG interfaces. Workaround: This scenario should resolve itself once the VSX linkup-delay timer expires.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what issue was resolved in Internal srvcs: Security PA infra Bug 278954?
original_answer: Internal srvcs: Security PA infra (Bug ID 278954): Symptom: Clients are onboarding with an incorrect auth-priority order. Users onboarded with a lower-priority authentication method, so higher-priority authentication requests were not seen. Scenario: When concurrent onboarding is enabled with a default authentication priority and non-default auth-precedence on a port, clients onboarding are taking the wrong auth-priority. Workaround: At the issue state, reconfigure the auth-priority on the port to onboard the clients.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what issue was resolved in Classifier Bug 276493?
original_answer: Classifier (Bug ID 276493): Symptom: A switch with a large policy set that applies correctly when initially configured from the CLI fails to bring up all interfaces after a reboot or upgrade. The switch shows the reason for this failure as a Policy init error. Scenario: The resources of the switch are split between the TCAM and the High capacity TCAM. This is illustrated via the show resources command. When a switch that is configured to use most of the TCAM resources via a large policy set and other features such as VSX enabled are rebooted or upgraded, the switch may not enable the policies and features in the same order that they were originally configured. This results in the TCAM resources being filled before all policies are enabled. Any policy that is attempted after the TCAM resources are fully utilized will not be implemented. Workaround: Manually remove and reapply the configuration in the original implemented order.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what issue was resolved in MPLS Bug 272036?
original_answer: MPLS (Bug ID 272036): Symptom: MPLS L3 VPN (each separate VRF) is unable to pass traffic when 8360 or 6400V2T is configured as PE. Scenario: Underlay network events occasionally causes the VPN to miss a route to VPN association events from the control plane. This causes the VPN to lose association with VRF and cause traffic loss in 8360 and 6400V2T. Workaround: "clear bgp \*" will help to resolve the issue. Platforms: 6400, 8360.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what issue was resolved in NTP Bug 271587?
original_answer: NTP (Bug ID 271587): Symptom: The NTP conductor won't be available to serve NTP clients on all interfaces for the VRF with both a client and conductor; instead, it will only listen on the configured source interface. Scenario: When the client and conductor are on the same VRF and the NTP source interface is configured for that VRF, it will affect which interface the NTP conductor listens on. Workaround: Maintain the conductor on a separate VRF.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what issue was resolved in TPM Bug 265125?
original_answer: TPM (Bug ID 265125): Symptom: Failure to establish or maintain a connection to Aruba Central. Scenario: A switch may fail to establish or maintain its relationship with Aruba Central. In these cases, the switch may display the "Central source connection status" field in the "show aruba-central" command will read "connection_failure". original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what issue was resolved in L3 Routes Bug 240831?
original_answer: L3 Routes (Bug ID 240831): Symptom: A route takes 180 seconds to get completely learned and stabilized when the feature enabled using the command route recursive default route is disabled during the route flap. Scenario: A route flap is observed upon any uplink failover scenarios when the nexthop of the BGP route tries to resolve via default route. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1000, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1000, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

reason: The original answer contains unclear wording and redundant information.
original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1001, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1001, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1001, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1001, what caveat is documented for BGP?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: BGP - In environments with VRRP or VSX peers, while performing mutual route leaking on the VRRP peers with BGP neighborship established in between and towards the upstream network, the switch will install both routes as ECMP instead of preferring the leaked route. Use route-maps to give lower/higher preference to the routes received from an iBGP peer. For example: Copy !

Use route-maps to give lower/higher preference to the routes received from an iBGP peer.

reason: The wording is clear and does not require any changes.
original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1001, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1010, what issue was resolved in VSX Bug 289568?
original_answer: VSX (Bug ID 289568): Symptom: The root switch initiates STP re-convergence and causes causing MAC flush and traffic loss. Scenario: This issue occurs during an upgrade process in VSX pair (which acts as non-root switch in RPVST topology). When the primary switch is down, this issue is encountered in a non-root access switch connected to this VSX-pair. Workaround: Install BPDU filter on the root-port of access switch during the software upgrade and remove the filter after the upgrade.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1010, what issue was resolved in IGMP/MLD Bug 292078?
original_answer: IGMP/MLD (Bug ID 292078): Symptom: The event message, Event ID 2628: “IGMP/MLD internal queue limit exceeded. Needs admin intervention” is logged multiple times. Scenario: This issue is observed in VXLAN overlay networks that do not have any multicast configuration. Workaround: Enable IGMP snooping on each VLAN carried in the overlay on all switches acting as VTEPs. Alternatively, use the logging filter command to deny log messages with the Event ID 2628.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1010, what issue was resolved in Central Bug 293314?
original_answer: Central (Bug ID 293314): Symptom: The client list is not populated in the Central client view page. Scenario: In very rare instances, after maintenance is performed on Aruba Central infrastructure, the client list in the Central client view page may fail to list the connected clients for some switches. This issue is uncommon enough that an upgrade to this release may not be warranted if the only reason for the upgrade is to avoid this potential issue. Workaround: From the switch console, enter bash via the start-shell command, and execute the command systemctl restart yang-resolverd.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1010, what issue was resolved in VSX Sync Bug 299838?
original_answer: VSX Sync (Bug ID 299838): Symptom: The interface VLAN configuration did not synchronize on the secondary switch. Scenario: This issue is observed in a pair of switches where the user configures an interface VLAN with vsx-sync configuration on the primary VSX and then creates the same interface VLAN on the secondary switch. Workaround: When vsx-sync is enabled on the interface VLAN of the primary VSX member, it is recommended to wait for at least 15 seconds before configuring the same on the secondary switch. Alternatively, configure the interface VLAN on the secondary switch and then enable vsx-sync on the primary switch.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1010, what issue was resolved in MAC Table Bug 287898?
original_answer: MAC Table (Bug ID 287898): Symptom: MAC addresses do not age out as expected in a chassis with different modules of line cards, and the l2mac-mgmrd process causes higher-than-expected CPU utilization. Scenario: Each line card in a chassis learns the full set of MAC addresses known to the system, including addresses associated with ports on other cards. In a situation where the line cards are not of one uniform type, with the same SKU, the system fails to remove the MAC address from the line cards that do not match the same SKU as the card where the MAC address was learned. Workaround: Manually clear the mac-address using the CLI command, clear mac-address <MAC-ADDR> [vlan <VLAN>] [confirm] and allow the l2mac-mgrd process usage to return to normal.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1010, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1020, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1020, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_answer: Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1020, what issue was resolved in Port Access Bug 301734?
original_answer: Port Access (Bug ID 301734): Symptom: The role assigned by CoA gets overridden by low-priority RADIUS assigned roles. Scenario: With default auth-priority set on a port, configure both MAC and 802.1X authentication. After a successful MAC authentication and concurrent onboarding, assign a role from the RADIUS server. While 802.1x is still in the authenticating state, assign a different RADIUS role using CoA and wait till the 802.1X gets deauthenticated. It can be observed that the role assigned by CoA gets overridden by the RADIUS assigned role. Workaround: Remove concurrent onboarding.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1020, what issue was resolved in VSX Bug 291905?
original_answer: VSX (Bug ID 291905): Symptom: Packet drop is observed and packets are not redirected to the other VSX member if the ISL LAG is reconfigured when the local MCLAG is down. Scenario: If the local MCLAG port on a VSX member is down and the MCLAG on the other VSX member is up, then traffic should be redirected over ISL to the VSX partner. If the ISL LAG is reconfigured, then the redirection will fail, causing packets headed toward that MCLAG to be dropped. Workaround: Remove and re-configure the ISL LAG and assign port membership before setting the LAG as ISL.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1020, what issue was resolved in ACL Logging Bug 305843?
original_answer: ACL Logging (Bug ID 305843): Symptom: Switch reloads unexpectedly during a firmware upgrade. Scenario: This issue occurs during an upgrade where a switch might reload while parsing an Access List (ACL) configuration. Workaround: It is recommended to follow the following procedure before the upgrade: Save the full configuration as a checkpoint using the copy running-config checkpoint <name> command. Remove all access-list configurations, save the configuration as the startup configuration using the copy running-config startup-config command. Upgrade the switch. Once the switch is upgraded, move the ACLs back to the running configuration by copying the saved checkpoint to the running configuration using the copy checkpoint <name> running-config command.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1020, what issue was resolved in Supportability Bug 311012?
original_answer: Supportability (Bug ID 311012): Symptom: High storage utilization is observed in the file system and as a result, users are unable to copy the support files. Scenario: Logs from various daemons are continuously generated and stored in multiple files in the /fs/logs/boot/ directory. This leads to a high storage utilization and prevents the copying of support files. Workaround: Use the directory mode support for file collection. After upgrading the device to the fixed firmware version, it is necessary to remove the previous boot logs. Performing two system reboots will clear these files.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1020, what issue was resolved in VSX Bug 298054?
original_answer: VSX (Bug ID 298054): Symptom: The MAC address learnt on the local VSX switch did not show up on the peer VSX switch. Scenario: This issue occurs when a member port is removed and another member port is added to the ISL lag during the same time. This issue is also observed when a physical port on the ISL lag is changed quickly. Workaround: Delete the existing ISL lag, recreate the LAG, add the ports to the LAG, and then set the LAG as ISL.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1020, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1030, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1030, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1030, what issue was resolved in Central Bug 300693?
original_answer: Central (Bug ID 300693): Symptom: Firmware updates via Aruba Central fails with an event log, Event | 4403 | LOG_ERR | CDTR | 1 | User admin: primary image update failed via HTTPS from common.cloud.hpe.com. Scenario: This issue is observed when the switch is configured to use http-proxy and the switch's upstream DNS server does not have an entry for common.cloud.hpe.com and when DNS recursion is disabled. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1030, what issue was resolved in DHCP Snooping Bug 312815?
original_answer: DHCP Snooping (Bug ID 312815): Symptom: Clients are unable to receive the IP address when DHCP snooping is enabled and when the source MAC address is different from the client hardware address. Scenario: Ethernet MAC address and client hardware address are diffrent for the clients. This issue is observed when DHCPv4 snooping drops the DHCP offer / DHCP acknowledgment packets when the source MAC address differs from the client MAC address field in the DHCP discover / request packets. This issue is seen even when the verify MAC option is disabled. Workaround: Configure the client hardware address as static MAC address in the MAC table.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1030, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1040, what issue was resolved in VSX Sync Bug 309500?
original_answer: VSX Sync (Bug ID 309500): Symptom: VSX-Sync is no longer syncing after a command for BGP to redistribute to OSPF with a route map is used. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1040, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1050, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1050, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1050, what issue was resolved in IGMP Bug 295438?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 295438): Symptom: IGMP/MLD control packets were not processed and the querier information was not learnt. Scenario: This issue is observed in a VXLAN setup without IGMP/MLD configurations where multicast control packets arrive continuously through the VLAN which does not have IGMP/MLD configuration. Workaround: Enable IGMP/MLD snooping on all the VLANs where multicast clients send IGMP/MLD control packets.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1050, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1060, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1060, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_answer: Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1060, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1070, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1070, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1070, what caveat is documented for SNMP?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1070, what caveat is documented for Certificates?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1070, what issue was resolved in BGP Bug 336108?
original_answer: BGP (Bug ID 336108): Symptom: BGP neighbor session is stuck in the connect state. Scenario: The BGP neighbor password was changed for the respective switches using a non-CLI method. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1070, what issue was resolved in Management Module Bug 336068?
original_answer: Management Module (Bug ID 336068): Symptom: Chassis line card encounters critical service fault and cannot recover with reboot. Scenario: In 8400 chassis, with an extremely high spike of incoming MAC events (learns, moves, removes), Critical Service Fault can occur due to an internal timeout while processing those events. The Critical Service Fault will cause the line card to reboot. If the same extremely high scale of incoming events is ongoing, this can cause the line card to timeout while initializing. Workaround: Temporarily isolate the chassis from the incoming events, allowing line card to successfully initialize.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1070, what issue was resolved in ARP Bug 335299?
original_answer: ARP (Bug ID 335299): Symptom: Client ARP entries are not aging out on the VSX peer switch. Scenario: When an ARP entry is learned and synced to the VSX peer, followed by a client IP change at the end host, the old ARP entries do not age out on the switch. Workaround: Manually clear the ARP entries on the respective VSX peer switch.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1070, what issue was resolved in ARP Bug 326400?
original_answer: ARP (Bug ID 326400): Symptom: Windows virtual machines detect duplicate IP addresses during Windows cluster failover. Scenario: During a Windows cluster failover, a Windows virtual machine sends an ARP probe for its own IP address to detect any duplicate IPs in the network. With ARP suppression enabled, the switch replies to the ARP probe instead of forwarding it through the VXLAN fabric. Workaround: Disable ARP suppression under the EVPN configuration.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1070, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1080, what issue was resolved in IGMP Bug 338204?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 338204): Symptom: An IPTV may experience a delay when changing channels. This problem may occur intermittently depending on the number of multicast entries. Scenario: The multicast entries present in the database need to be updated with the activity-status every 30 seconds. The activity-status need to be fetched from the hardware for all the multicast entries. This is taking 5-6 seconds of time in the customer network. So, the channel change, which is leading to the forwarding port change in the hardware, is pending behind the update of activity-status of multicast entries.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1080, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1090, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1090, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_answer: Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1090, what caveat is documented for SNMP?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1090, what caveat is documented for Certificates?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

original_answer: SNMP (Bug ID 335246): Symptom: Delay of ~30s observed when updating VLAN access config via snmpset and verifying via snmpget immediately after. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1090, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what caveat is documented for SNMP?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what caveat is documented for Certificates?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what issue was resolved in ACL Bug 234200?
original_answer: ACL (Bug ID 234200): Symptom: After restoring a checkpoint on the VSX primary switch that contains ACLs configured with vsx-sync, the ACL fails to apply on the VSX secondary switch and is not functional. Warning messages appear in the configuration, but no event logs are generated to indicate the ACL application failure. As a result, traffic that should be blocked by the ACL continues to pass through the secondary switch. Scenario: This issue occurs in the following scenario: 1. A VSX pair is configured. 2. ACLs are configured on the primary switch with vsx-sync enabled. 3. The ACLs are applied to interfaces or VLANs with vsx-sync enabled. 4. A checkpoint is created with this configuration. 5. The checkpoint is later restored on the primary switch. When examining the configuration on the secondary switch after checkpoint restoration, warning messages similar to the following appear: access-list ip list1 vsx-sync ! 10 deny any any any count ! access-list ip list1 user configuration does not match active configuration. ! run 'access-list TYPE NAME reset' or 'access-list all reset' to reset the ACL to match the active configuration. Workaround: Reapply the ACL on the primary switch to each interface where it is applied. This triggers proper synchronization to the secondary switch. The following is an example of proper synchronization: vlan 110 apply access-list ip list1 out vlan 120 apply access-list ip list1 out vlan 130 apply access-list ip list1 out.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what issue was resolved in BGP Bug 360748?
original_answer: BGP (Bug ID 360748): Symptom: BGP selects the wrong path, causing routing issues. Scenario: In some scenarios BGP incorrectly selects the path with the highest AS-Path. This causes incorrect paths to be propagated to the Routing Information Base (RIB) and Forwarding Information Base (FIB), potentially causing routing issues. Workaround: Lower the local preference of EVPN originated router. This causes the higher local preference route to be selected.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what issue was resolved in VXLAN Bug 359279?
original_answer: VXLAN (Bug ID 359279): Symptom: After a virtual machine is migrated (vMotion) from one data center to another in a static VXLAN environment, the VM cannot reach its gateway or external destinations, resulting in ping timeouts and connection failures. Scenario: In some scenarios, the switch can fail to program tunnel to VNI associations, leading to traffic loss. Workaround: Remove the affected VNI from the configuration on the switches in the source data center and then add the VNI to the configuration again.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what issue was resolved in DHCP Relay Bug 359666?
original_answer: DHCP Relay (Bug ID 359666): Symptom: With hpe-relay core dump legacy bootp client does not get IP. Scenario: When the DHCP relay daemon processes a legacy BOOTP client packet that lacks the option-53 field, it fails to properly validate the packet's structure before attempting to access this field, leading to a crash. This issue occurs exclusively when the smart-relay feature is enabled. Workaround: Disable the global DHCP smart-relay configuration if not required using no dhcp-smart-relay.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what issue was resolved in Multicast Bug 338204?
original_answer: Multicast (Bug ID 338204): Symptom: IPTV may experience a delay when changing channels. This problem may occur intermittently depending on the number of multicast entries. Scenario: The multicast entries present in the database need to be updated with the activity-status every 30 seconds. The activity-status need to be fetched from the hardware for all multicast entries. This is taking 5-6 seconds of time and delays the channel change, which leads to the forwarding port change in the hardware. Workaround: The multicast activity-status update is improved to fetch the status from all of the LCs at the same time instead of fetching it one-by-one from each of the LCs.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what issue was resolved in Multicast Bug 342612?
original_answer: Multicast (Bug ID 342612): Symptom: When switching between IPTV channels, there is an intermittent delay of 2-6 seconds before the new video stream is displayed to the end user. Scenario: The system experiences a small delay in the program of a new multicast flow after receiving the IGMP report. During this time, critical initial video packets containing SPS (Sequence Parameter Set) and PPS (Picture Parameter Set) information may be dropped. These packets are essential for the decoder to properly display the H.264 video stream. Workaround: Configure static IGMP groups for the multicast streams using static igmp join <multicast-group-address> interface <decoder-port>. This ensures the hardware is pre-programmed with the multicast flows, eliminating the join latency.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what issue was resolved in VSX Bug 355409?
original_answer: VSX (Bug ID 355409): Symptom: An egress loop can be seen leading to replicated BUM traffic whenever the LC reboots in a VSX setup. Scenario: In some scenarios, after a line card reboot in a VSX setup an egress loop can be seen leading to replicated BUM traffic whenever the LC reboots in a VSX setup. Workaround: Reboot the switch or trigger an mm failover to force the VSX configuration reporgramming in the hardware.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what issue was resolved in REST Bug 353178?
original_answer: REST (Bug ID 353178): Symptom: When uploading firmware using the Web UI or REST API, the system displays the following error message despite the firmware being successfully transferred to the device: There was an error uploading new firmware. Firmware image is invalid. Please enter a valid firmware image. Scenario: When logging console is enabled in the switch configuration and a user attempts to upload firmware through the Web UI or REST API, the upload process is completed, but an HTTP 500 error is generated. Workaround: Until upgrading to the fixed version, use one of the following methods: 1. Disable console logging by removing logging console from the configuration. Then attempt the firmware upload using the Web UI or REST API again. 2. Use TFTP for firmware upload by uploading the firmware using TFTP or SFTP instead of the Web UI or REST API. Example command: copy tftp/sftp://[server-ip]/[firmware-file] primary vrf mgmt.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1110, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1110, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_answer: ARP (Bug ID 351751): Symptom: After a server failover, the new MAC address is not updated on the remote VTEP VSX secondary node. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1110, what issue was resolved in BGP Bug 360748?
original_answer: BGP (Bug ID 360748): Symptom: BGP selects the wrong path, causing routing issues. Scenario: In some scenarios BGP incorrectly selects the path with the highest AS-Path. This causes incorrect paths to be propagated to the Routing Information Base (RIB) and Forwarding Information Base (FIB), potentially causing routing issues. Workaround: Lower the local preference of EVPN originated router. The higher local preference route is selected.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1110, what issue was resolved in SNMP Bug 367181?
original_answer: SNMP (Bug ID 367181): Symptom: Switches may fail to respond to SNMP queries after upgrading from 10.08 version of code to 10.13 code. Scenario: This issue occurs when a switch has been configured with snmp-server response-source prior to upgrade. Workaround: After upgrading, access the switch shell and either: 1. Delete the file /fs/nos/net-snmp/agent-swns/snmp.conf, or 2. Edit the file to comment out (with #) the line containing [snmp] srcipaddr entry 3. Restart the SNMP service with systemctl restart snmpd-wrapper-in-c.service.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1110, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1120, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1120, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1120, what issue was resolved in Logging Bug 358847?
original_answer: Logging (Bug ID 358847): Symptom: A VSF stack member switch unexpectedly reboots due to the ops-switchd process crashing with a signal:6 error. Scenario: In some scenarios with excessive ACL event logging, a crash may occur due to a failure in the system when processing a high volume of ACL log entries. Workaround: Minimize the use of the log keyword in ACL entries, especially on high-traffic rules. Consider using ACL counters instead of logging for monitoring traffic patterns.

original_answer: EVPN (Bug ID 364576): Symptom: Missing ARP entries showing as "incomplete" in the ARP table, causing connectivity issues in remote VTEP. original_answer: NTP (Bug ID 371717): Symptom: Excessive connection lost and connection established logging when multiple NTP servers are configured. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what issue was resolved in EVPN Bug 379727?
original_answer: EVPN (Bug ID 379727): Symptom: There is a resource leak when an L3 host (ARP / ND) moves between local port (non-VxLAN) to VxLAN tunnel. Over a period of time and especially under scaled L3 hosts environments, this slow resource leak may lead the system to run out of L3 resources critical for neighbor and route forwarding, leading to L3 connectivity issues. Scenario: With VxLAN deployments, if a host moves between a local port to VxLAN tunnel, there is a critical resource leak with every host move. When there are a large number of mobile wireless devices changing wireless access point connections, specifically between locally connected (non-VxLAN) and over VxLAN tunnels, L3 resources needed for neighbor / route forwarding may run out. Workaround: Reboot the box to reclaim leaked resources. In a VSX deploymeny, reboot the secondary device, then reboot the primary device after the secondary device comes back up to keep downtime minimal.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

Scenario: This issue can occur with these following time sensitive steps: 1. Container A is created, Container Manager is processing the creation of said container. 2. A delete request for container A is sent using CLI or REST. 3. A request to create container A is sent. 4. When checking for statuses using REST or show container, the container is marked as down. Workaround: Where possible, avoid deleting and recreating the same container while it is being created, allow it to reach the operational state. If the issue is encountered, delete the container using REST or CLI and restart the switch to release the occupied container resources.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1160, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1160, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_answer: Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1160, what known issue is documented for PBR Bug 277126?
original_answer: PBR (Bug ID 277126): Symptom: Classifier or PBR Policy is not enforced for traffic coming via ISL link. Scenario: In a VSX+ VRRP topology, if a policy is applied on SVI, all packets which reach the VRRP secondary device go to the primary device via the ISL. All of those packets are not getting the classifier policy enforced. Workaround: To prevent packets from being forwarded to VSX primary via ISL, use active gateway instead of VRRP. This provides better active-active redundancy and packets reaching either VSX switch process the packet destined to active gateway.

original_answer: PBR (Bug ID 277126): In a VSX+ VRRP topology, if a policy is applied on SVI, all packets which reach the VRRP secondary device go to the primary device via the ISL. All of those packets are not getting the classifier policy enforced.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1160, what is the workaround for PBR Bug 277126?
original_answer: PBR (Bug ID 277126): The documented workaround is: To prevent packets from being forwarded to VSX primary via ISL, use active gateway instead of VRRP. This provides better active-active redundancy and packets reaching either VSX switch process the packet destined to active gateway.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1161, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1161, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what issue was resolved in MAC Tables Bug 400513?
original_answer: MAC Tables (Bug ID 400513): Symptom: MACs are only showing on one side of a VSX pair. For instance, a MAC is learned on a physical port on one VSX device and is missing from the peer VSX device. Scenario: This issue can occur resolving a network loop which causes the MACs to quickly move from one VSX device to the other, particularly when MACs are moving from EVPN tunnels to physical ports. Workaround: Using the CLI to clear the problematic MAC address.

cleaned_answer: MAC Tables (Bug ID 400513): Symptom: MAC addresses are only showing on one side of a VSX pair. For example, a MAC is learned on a physical port on one VSX device and is missing from the peer VSX device. Scenario: This issue can occur when resolving a network loop, causing MACs to quickly move between VSX devices, especially when MACs are moving from EVPN tunnels to physical ports. Workaround: Use the CLI to clear the problematic MAC address.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what issue was resolved in PBR Bug 407418?
original_answer: PBR (Bug ID 407418): Symptom: Classifier Policy is not enforced for traffic coming from an ISL link. Scenario: For a topology with VSX+ VRRP, if the policy is applied on SVI, all packets which reach the VRRP secondary device go to the primary device via the ISL. All of those packets are not getting classifier policy enforced. Even though the policy action is PBR redirect, those packets are not getting redirected. Workaround: Two workarounds are possible to prevent packets from being forwarded to VSX primary via ISL. 1. Use active gateway to give better active active redundancy. Packets reaching either VSX device process the packet destined to active gateway. 2. Turn off the interface connection from VRRP secondary device to down switch. This method may cause redundancy and is not preferred.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what issue was resolved in CPU Rx Bug 411613?
original_answer: CPU Rx (Bug ID 411613): Symptom: Several protocols, such as ROP port, LLDP, and LACP, do not work. Scenario: This issue may occur if no module configuration is issued to any LC and the LC is restored or a different LC with module is configured, the previous configuration applied to the system does not recover, even though the same configuration was working prior to no module. Workaround: Reboot the switch. If reboot is not possible, and LC needs be swapped, remove any configuration on the LC before performing no module.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what issue was resolved in IGMP Bug 215677?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 215677): Symptom: IGMP/MLD configuration changes do not take effect when the hpe-mgmdd process is flooded with IGMP/MLD control packets. Scenario: In a situation where IGMP or MLD control packets (Joins or Leaves) are part of a loop, or if misbehaving clients send continuous streams of IGMP/MLD control packets, the hpe-mgmdd daemon's packet queue can become continuously full. Workaround: Restart the MGMD daemon using the command systemctl restart hpe-mgmdd.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what issue was resolved in VSX-Sync Bug 299838?
original_answer: VSX-Sync (Bug ID 299838): Symptom: An Interface VLAN configuration is not getting synced to secondary switch. Scenario: This issue can occur n a pair of switches in VSX configuration, when the user creates an Interface VLAN with VSX-Sync configuration in primary switch, then the user creates the same Interface VLAN on secondary, but the configuration is not synced. Workaround: Once VSX-Sync has been enabled on primary Interface VLAN, wait ~15 seconds before creating the Interface VLAN on the secondary interface. Another workaround is to first create the Interface VLAN on the secondary and then enable VSX-Sync on the primary Interface VLAN. (There is no need to wait in this second workaround).

Workaround: Once VSX-Sync has been enabled on the primary Interface VLAN, wait ~15 seconds before creating the Interface VLAN on the secondary interface. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what issue was resolved in VSX Bug 299851?
original_answer: VSX (Bug ID 299851): Symptom: The VSX software upgrade software version is not properly validated when upgrading using TFTP. Scenario: When upgrading using TFTP, it is possible to load mismatched software versions, resulting in a VSX upgrade failure and a VSX software mismatch between the primary and secondary VSX nodes. Workaround: Confirm that software image versions are compatible. Software upgrades using boot banks rather than TFTP will perform a proper version check before completing the upgrade.

reason: The original question and answer are clear but could be more concise.
original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.0007, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.0007, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will be disconnected. This cycle of connecting and disconnecting continues until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To prevent this unnecessary reconnection, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.0007, what caveat is documented for Certificates?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what issue was resolved in VSX-Sync Bug 309500?
original_answer: VSX-Sync (Bug ID 309500): Symptom: VSX sync is no longer syncing after a configuring BGP to redistribute to OSPF with a route map. Scenario: This issue can occur on a pair of switches in a VSX configuration that are syncing for BGP when a user configures redistribution for BGP to OSPF with a route map on the primary switch. VSX sync will not sync the redistribution configuration or any other new configuration. Workaround: Disable BGP syncing or remove the redistribution for BGP to OSPF with a route map.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.1001, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.1001, what issue was resolved in VSX-Sync Bug 309500?
original_answer: VSX-Sync (Bug ID 309500): Symptom: VSX sync is no longer syncing after a configuring BGP to redistribute to OSPF with a route map. Scenario: This issue can occur on a pair of switches in a VSX configuration that are syncing for BGP when a user configures redistribution for BGP to OSPF with a route map on the primary switch. VSX sync will not sync the redistribution configuration or any other new configuration. Workaround: Disable BGP syncing or remove the redistribution for BGP to OSPF with a route map.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.1010, what issue was resolved in IGMP Bug 295438?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 295438): Symptom: IGMP/MLD control packets were not processed and the querier information was not learnt. Scenario: This issue is observed in a VXLAN setup without IGMP/MLD configurations where multicast control packets arrive continuously through the VLAN which does not have IGMP/MLD configuration. Workaround: Enable IGMP/MLD snooping on all the VLANs where multicast clients send IGMP/MLD control packets.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.1010, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.1020, what issue was resolved in Spanning Tree Bug 329044?
original_answer: Spanning Tree (Bug ID 329044): Symptom: The RPVST daemon crashes unexpectedly. Scenario: This issue is observed when an STP-aware switch is connected to a port where spanning-tree guard is configured. Workaround: Avoid connecting STP-aware switches to spanning-tree guard enabled ports. Alternatively, remove the BPDU-guard configuration to temporarily avoid further crashes.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what issue was resolved in Management Module Bug 336068?
original_answer: Management Module (Bug ID 336068): Symptom: Chassis line card encounters critical service fault and cannot recover with reboot. Scenario: In 8400 chassis, with an extremely high spike of incoming MAC events (learns, moves, removes), Critical Service Fault can occur due to an internal timeout while processing those events. The Critical Service Fault will cause the line card to reboot. If the same extremely high scale of incoming events is ongoing, this can case the line card to again timeout while initializing. Workaround: Temporarily isolate the chassis from the incoming "storm" of events, allowing line card to successfully initialize.
cleaned_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what issue was resolved in the Management Module Bug 336068?
cleaned_answer: Management Module (Bug ID 336068): Symptom: Chassis line card encounters critical service fault and cannot recover with reboot. The Critical Service Fault will cause the line card to reboot. If the same extremely high scale of incoming events is ongoing, this can cause the line card to again timeout while initializing. Workaround: Temporarily isolate the chassis from the incoming 'storm' of events, allowing the line card to successfully initialize.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what issue was resolved in ARP Bug 326400?
original_answer: ARP (Bug ID 326400): Symptom: Windows virtual machines detect duplicate IP addresses during Windows cluster failover. Scenario: During a Windows cluster failover, a Windows virtual machine sends an ARP probe for its own IP address to detect any duplicate IPs in the network. With ARP suppression enabled, the switch replies to the ARP probe instead of forwarding it through the VXLAN fabric. Workaround: Disable ARP suppression under the EVPN configuration.

With ARP suppression enabled, the switch replies to the ARP probe instead of forwarding it through the VXLAN fabric. Workaround: Disable ARP suppression under the EVPN configuration.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.1040, what issue was resolved in NTP Bug 341465?
original_answer: NTP (Bug ID 341465): Symptom: Switch is unable to sync with NTP server even though NTP server is reachable and responding to the client. Scenario: In some scenarios when connecting to a NTP server that uses special characters for the reference ID field. The NTP client device will not be able to sync with the server and the error Delimiter for jitter error will be displayed. Workaround: Connect to an NTP server that doesn't uses special characters for the reference ID fields.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.1040, what issue was resolved in BGP Bug 338484?
original_answer: BGP (Bug ID 338484): Symptom: BGP session fails to establish. When BGP is configured with the AO (Authentication Option) command, the session remains stuck in the "Connect" state if either peer is rebooted. Scenario: This issue occurs when TCP Authentication Option (TCPAO) is configured with a peer-group. Workaround: Delete and re-apply the config or use individual peer instead of peer-group.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.1040, what issue was resolved in IGMP Bug 338204?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 338204): Symptom: An IPTV may experience a delay when changing channels. This problem may occur intermittently depending on the number of multicast entries. Scenario: The multicast entries present in the database need to be updated with the activity-status every 30 seconds. The activity-status need to be fetched from the hardware for all the multicast entries. This is taking 5-6 seconds of time in the customer network. So, the channel change, which is leading to the forwarding port change in the hardware, is pending behind the update of activity-status of multicast entries.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.1040, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what issue was resolved in MSTP Bug 346938?
original_answer: MSTP (Bug ID 346938): Symptom: Intermittent MSTP/RPVST starvation messages observed coming from source mac address 00:00:00:00:00:00. Scenario: When 3rd party legacy devices send packets with source mac address 00:00:00:00:00:00 to CX devices intermittent BPDU starvation might be observed. Workaround: Configure ACL to drop invalid source mac address 00:00:00:00:00:00 in all switches where end devices are connected.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what issue was resolved in VXLAN Bug 339717?
original_answer: VXLAN (Bug ID 339717): Symptom: ops-switchd crash while collecting VXLAN diagdump or collecting support files all. Scenario: In some VXLAN scenarios with multiple LAGs and multiple overlay VLAN on those lags. After device reboot, the LAG interface could show incorrect VXLAN Programming. If the user attempts to remove VLANs, VNI-to-VXLAN configurations that belong to the affected LAGS, a ops-switchd crash might be observed. Workaround: Ensure LAG interfaces are up before configuring the VLANs for the LAG interface.
