original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what issue was resolved in ACL Bug 234200?
original_answer: ACL (Bug ID 234200): Symptom: After restoring a checkpoint on the VSX primary switch that contains ACLs configured with vsx-sync, the ACL fails to apply on the VSX secondary switch and is not functional. Warning messages appear in the configuration, but no event logs are generated to indicate the ACL application failure. As a result, traffic that should be blocked by the ACL continues to pass through the secondary switch. Scenario: This issue occurs in the following scenario: 1. A VSX pair is configured. 2. ACLs are configured on the primary switch with vsx-sync enabled. 3. The ACLs are applied to interfaces or VLANs with vsx-sync enabled. 4. A checkpoint is created with this configuration. 5. The checkpoint is later restored on the primary switch. When examining the configuration on the secondary switch after checkpoint restoration, warning messages similar to the following appear: access-list ip list1 vsx-sync ! 10 deny any any any count ! access-list ip list1 user configuration does not match active configuration. ! run 'access-list TYPE NAME reset' or 'access-list all reset' to reset the ACL to match the active configuration. Workaround: Reapply the ACL on the primary switch to each interface where it is applied. This triggers proper synchronization to the secondary switch. The following is an example of proper synchronization: vlan 110 apply access-list ip list1 out vlan 120 apply access-list ip list1 out vlan 130 apply access-list ip list1 out.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what issue was resolved in VXLAN Bug 359279?
original_answer: VXLAN (Bug ID 359279): Symptom: After a virtual machine is migrated (vMotion) from one data center to another in a static VXLAN environment, the VM cannot reach its gateway or external destinations, resulting in ping timeouts and connection failures. Scenario: In some scenarios, the switch can fail to program tunnel to VNI associations, leading to traffic loss. Workaround: Remove the affected VNI from the configuration on the switches in the source data center and then add the VNI to the configuration again.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what issue was resolved in DHCP Relay Bug 359666?
original_answer: DHCP Relay (Bug ID 359666): Symptom: With hpe-relay core dump legacy bootp client does not get IP. Scenario: When the DHCP relay daemon processes a legacy BOOTP client packet that lacks the option-53 field, it fails to properly validate the packet's structure before attempting to access this field, leading to a crash. This issue occurs exclusively when the smart-relay feature is enabled. Workaround: Disable the global DHCP smart-relay configuration if not required using no dhcp-smart-relay.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what issue was resolved in Multicast Bug 342612?
original_answer: Multicast (Bug ID 342612): Symptom: When switching between IPTV channels, there is an intermittent delay of 2-6 seconds before the new video stream is displayed to the end user. Scenario: The system experiences a small delay in the program of a new multicast flow after receiving the IGMP report. During this time, critical initial video packets containing SPS (Sequence Parameter Set) and PPS (Picture Parameter Set) information may be dropped. These packets are essential for the decoder to properly display the H.264 video stream. Workaround: Configure static IGMP groups for the multicast streams using static igmp join <multicast-group-address> interface <decoder-port>. This ensures the hardware is pre-programmed with the multicast flows, eliminating the join latency.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what issue was resolved in VSX Bug 355409?
original_answer: VSX (Bug ID 355409): Symptom: An egress loop can be seen leading to replicated BUM traffic whenever the LC reboots in a VSX setup. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what issue was resolved in REST Bug 353178?
original_answer: REST (Bug ID 353178): Symptom: When uploading firmware using the Web UI or REST API, the system displays the following error message despite the firmware being successfully transferred to the device: There was an error uploading new firmware. Firmware image is invalid. Please enter a valid firmware image. Scenario: When logging console is enabled in the switch configuration and a user attempts to upload firmware through the Web UI or REST API, the upload process is completed, but an HTTP 500 error is generated. Workaround: Until upgrading to the fixed version, use one of the following methods: 1. Disable console logging by removing logging console from the configuration. Then attempt the firmware upload using the Web UI or REST API again. 2. Use TFTP for firmware upload by uploading the firmware using TFTP or SFTP instead of the Web UI or REST API. Example command: copy tftp/sftp://[server-ip]/[firmware-file] primary vrf mgmt.
cleaned_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what issue was resolved in REST Bug 353178?

Workaround: Until upgrading to the fixed version, disable console logging by removing 'logging console' from the configuration and attempt the firmware upload again, or use TFTP for firmware upload.

original_answer: DHCP (Bug ID 348727): Symptom: DHCP server configuration status becomes stuck in a pending state after configuration changes are pushed from Aruba Central, preventing proper DHCP service operation. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what issue was resolved in VLANs Bug 348612?
original_answer: VLANs (Bug ID 348612): Symptom: When wireless clients roam between access points connected to the same switch, they lose connectivity and receive an APIPA address. Connectivity is only restored after the MAC-age timer expires. Scenario: When a client is authenticated with a VLAN on a secure port, the VLAN is added as an ingress-only VLAN on other secure ports, but the MSTP state for those ports remains in blocked state instead of forwarding state, preventing seamless client movement. Workaround: Reduce the MAC-age timer to minimize the impact. or configure AP ports in device-mode instead of client-mode as follows: interface 1/1/X port-access client-limit 1 port-access client-mode device.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.0001, what issue was resolved in Activate Bug 317632?
original_answer: Activate (Bug ID 317632): Symptom: The switch contacts the Google Public DNS server: 8.8.8.8. Scenario: This issue can occur if there is no user configuration for DNS server nor DNS hosts and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured. The switch uses the Google DNS server 8.8.8.8 by default. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms. Workaround: Disable HPE Aruba Networking Central functionality.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.0001, what issue was resolved in Active Gateway Bug 319555?
original_answer: Active Gateway (Bug ID 319555): Symptom: Configuring the same MAC address for both VSX system-mac and active-gateway MAC can cause internal potential network issues. Scenario: Configuring the same MAC address for VSX system-mac and active-gateway MAC leads to conflicts. The system-mac VALUE, VALUE-1, and VALUE+1 are used for VSX internal processes, particularly in relation to spanning-tree. Any MAC address collision with system-mac may have adverse side effects. Workaround: Ensure that the active-gateway MAC address is different from the VSX system-mac address. Take care to avoid reusing the system-mac address for the active-gateway.

cleaned_answer: Active Gateway (Bug ID 319555): The symptom is: Configuring the same MAC address for both VSX system-mac and active-gateway MAC can cause internal potential network issues..

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.0001, what issue was resolved in DHCP Snooping Bug 325316?
original_answer: DHCP Snooping (Bug ID 325316): Symptom: A critical log message is generated for a ipsavd process daemon restart with signal 11. Scenario: This daemon restart will be observed when a DHCP client sends a DHCP renew packet without the end option when dhcpv4-snooping option 82 is disabled. Workaround: Enable dhcpv4-snooping option 82, or configure a maximum lease-time for the client (365 days) to avoid this issue.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.0001, what issue was resolved in EVPN/BGP Bug 307091?
original_answer: EVPN/BGP (Bug ID 307091): Symptom: The EVPN route selection process may cause an suboptimal path selected. Scenario: During the EVPN best path selection, the IGP metric to reach the nexthop of the EVPN route may not be taken into the consideration. Workaround: Other BGP attributes (like Local preference or weight, etc.) need to be altered for the routes to select the desired path. However this is may not be feasible in all the routing policy/scenarios for the enterprise.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.0001, what issue was resolved in Spanning Tree Bug 329044?
original_answer: Spanning Tree (Bug ID 329044): Symptom: An RPVST daemon daemon restart occurred. Scenario: This issue can occur when connecting an STP-aware switch to a port where spanning-tree guard is configured. Ensure that the peer switch has an RPVST VLAN that is not configured on the switch. Workaround: Refrain from connecting STP-aware switches to these ports. Alternatively, temporarily remove the BPDU-guard configuration to prevent further daemon restarts.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.0001, what issue was resolved in VSX Bug 292867?
original_answer: VSX (Bug ID 292867): Symptom: A VSX software upgrade can take more than 20 minutes to complete. Scenario: This issue can occur during a VSX software upgrade on a VXLAN deployment without IGMP/MLD running on a few VLANs associated with a VNI, when multicast control packets are coming in continuously through the VLAN. Workaround: Enable IGMP/MLD snooping on all the VLANs where multicast clients are sending IGMP/MLD control packets.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.0001, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.0001, what known issue is documented for RADIUS Port-Access Bug 320342?
original_answer: RADIUS Port-Access (Bug ID 320342): Symptom: The RADIUS server hostname will get updated to the renewed IP addresses found in DNS server. Scenario: The problem is seen when the host addresses in DNS servers get updated against the hostnames, but the Switch fails to refresh its cache with the renewed address. Workaround: The default refresh timer configured in the switch is 300 seconds. A network administrator can configure a custom value according to their needs after which the switch would automatically update the host addresses of the RADIUS server in its cache.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what issue was resolved in Active Gateway Bug 319555?
original_answer: Active Gateway (Bug ID 319555): Symptom: Configuring the same MAC address for both VSX system-mac and active-gateway MAC can cause internal potential network issues. Scenario: Configuring the same MAC address for VSX system-mac and active-gateway MAC leads to conflicts. The system-mac VALUE, VALUE-1, and VALUE+1 are used for VSX internal processes, particularly in relation to spanning-tree. Any MAC address collision with system-mac may have adverse side effects. Workaround: Ensure that the active-gateway MAC address is different from the VSX system-mac address. Take care to avoid reusing the system-mac address for the active-gateway.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what issue was resolved in DHCP Snooping Bug 325316?
original_answer: DHCP Snooping (Bug ID 325316): Symptom: A critical log message is generated for a ipsavd process daemon restart with signal 11. Scenario: This daemon restart will be observed when a DHCP client sends a DHCP renew packet without the end option when dhcpv4-snooping option 82 is disabled. Workaround: Enable dhcpv4-snooping option 82, or configure a maximum lease-time for the client (365 days) to avoid this issue.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what issue was resolved in EVPN/BGP Bug 307091?
original_answer: EVPN/BGP (Bug ID 307091): Symptom: The EVPN route selection process may cause an suboptimal path selected. Scenario: During the EVPN best path selection, the IGP metric to reach the nexthop of the EVPN route may not be taken into the consideration. Workaround: Other BGP attributes (like Local preference or weight, etc.) need to be altered for the routes to select the desired path. However this is may not be feasible in all the routing policy/scenarios for the enterprise.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what issue was resolved in SNMP Bug 317831?
original_answer: SNMP (Bug ID 317831): Symptom: When configuring a VLAN outside of the VLAN range through REST, the hpe-entityd process daemon restarts. Scenario: When a VLAN configured through REST is outside the range of allowable VLAN values, hpe-entityd daemon restarts when attempting to match ports to currently configured VLANs. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what issue was resolved in Spanning Tree Bug 329044?
original_answer: Spanning Tree (Bug ID 329044): Symptom: An RPVST daemon daemon restart occurred. Scenario: This issue can occur when connecting an STP-aware switch to a port where spanning-tree guard is configured. Ensure that the peer switch has an RPVST VLAN that is not configured on the switch. Workaround: Refrain from connecting STP-aware switches to these ports. Alternatively, temporarily remove the BPDU-guard configuration to prevent further daemon restarts.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what issue was resolved in VSX Bug 292867?
original_answer: VSX (Bug ID 292867): Symptom: A VSX software upgrade can take more than 20 minutes to complete. Scenario: This issue can occur during a VSX software upgrade on a VXLAN deployment without IGMP/MLD running on a few VLANs associated with a VNI, when multicast control packets are coming in continuously through the VLAN. Workaround: Enable IGMP/MLD snooping on all the VLANs where multicast clients are sending IGMP/MLD control packets.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what known issue is documented for RADIUS Port-Access Bug 320342?
original_answer: RADIUS Port-Access (Bug ID 320342): Symptom: The RADIUS server hostname will get updated to the renewed IP addresses found in DNS server. Scenario: The problem is seen when the host addresses in DNS servers get updated against the hostnames, but the Switch fails to refresh its cache with the renewed address. Workaround: The default refresh timer configured in the switch is 300 seconds. A network administrator can configure a custom value according to their needs after which the switch would automatically update the host addresses of the RADIUS server in its cache.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1005, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1010, what issue was resolved in Mirroring Bug 342437?
original_answer: Mirroring (Bug ID 342437): Symptom: Packets near MTU limits in size may not be present in a port or tunnel/remote based mirror. Scenario: When a client configures a mirror where the destination interface has the same MTU size, mirrored packets that are close to max MTU are incorrectly dropped and not mirrored. This applies to remote/tunnel mirrors as well. Workaround: Configuring mirror destination ports as routing port and set the IP MTU size. For a VLAN interface, set the IP MTU on the VLAN SVI. IP MTU must be set to accommodate the ethernet overhead +tag. For the default MTU, 1500, the IP MTU would be 1518. For a tunneled scenario, the MTU must also account for the tunnel header.
cleaned_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1010, what issue was resolved in Mirroring Bug 342437?
cleaned_answer: Mirroring (Bug ID 342437): Symptom: Packets near MTU limits in size may not be present in a port or tunnel/remote based mirror. Scenario: When a client configures a mirror where the destination interface has the same MTU size, mirrored packets that are close to max MTU are incorrectly dropped and not mirrored. This applies to remote/tunnel mirrors as well. Workaround: Configuring mirror destination ports as routing port and set the IP MTU size. For a VLAN interface, set the IP MTU on the VLAN SVI. IP MTU must be set to accommodate the ethernet overhead +tag. For the default MTU, 1500, the IP MTU would be 1518. For a tunneled scenario, the MTU must also account for the tunnel header.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1010, what issue was resolved in VXLAN Bug 339717?
original_answer: VXLAN (Bug ID 339717): Symptom: ops-switchd crash while collecting VXLAN diagdump or collecting support files all. Scenario: In some VXLAN scenarios with multiple LAGs and multiple overlay VLAN on those lags. After device reboot, the LAG interface could show incorrect VXLAN Programming. Workaround: Ensure LAG interfaces are up before configuring the VLANs for the lag.

If the user attempts to remove VLANs, VNI-to-VXLAN configurations that belong to the affected LAGs might cause an ops-switchd crash. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1010, what issue was resolved in ACL Bug 234200?
original_answer: ACL (Bug ID 234200): Symptom: ACL fails to apply only the VSX secondary switch. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1010, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.
cleaned_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1010, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
cleaned_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1010, what known issue is documented for DHCP Bug 405873?
original_answer: DHCP (Bug ID 405873): Symptom: When modifying DHCP Server pool parameters (such as DNS server entries) using Aruba Central template-based configuration, the changes are accepted without warning but do not take effect. The running DHCP configuration continues using the previous values. Scenario: Configuration updates to a DHCP Server pool require the DHCP Server to be disabled and re-enabled (or the switch rebooted) before the new settings become active. When these same changes are pushed through Central templates, they do not take effect. Workaround: For CX software versions prior to 10.15.1020, after making any DHCP server pool configuration change, perform one of the following to activate the new configuration Disable and re-enable the DHCP server instance, or Restart the DHCP server process, or Reboot the switch.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1020, what issue was resolved in VSX Bug 341536?
original_answer: VSX (Bug ID 341536): Symptom: Traffic does not flow over MCLAGs, causing the Egress Block to remain unset. This results in traffic from the ISL being sent out over the MCLAG, potentially causing connected devices to receive duplicate packets. Scenario: When configuring an MCAG with AFC, traffic does not flow over MCLAGs. Workaround: Create a new VLAN on the devices where the MCLAG was configured.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1020, what issue was resolved in ACL Bug 234200?
original_answer: ACL (Bug ID 234200): Symptom: After restoring a checkpoint on the VSX primary switch that contains ACLs configured with vsx-sync, the ACL fails to apply on the VSX secondary switch and is not functional. Warning messages appear in the configuration, but no event logs are generated to indicate the ACL application failure. As a result, traffic that should be blocked by the ACL continues to pass through the secondary switch. Scenario: This issue occurs in the following scenario: 1. A VSX pair is configured. 2. ACLs are configured on the primary switch with vsx-sync enabled. 3. The ACLs are applied to interfaces or VLANs with vsx-sync enabled. 4. A checkpoint is created with this configuration. 5. The checkpoint is later restored on the primary switch. When examining the configuration on the secondary switch after checkpoint restoration, warning messages similar to the following appear: access-list ip list1 vsx-sync ! 10 deny any any any count ! access-list ip list1 user configuration does not match active configuration. ! run 'access-list TYPE NAME reset' or 'access-list all reset' to reset the ACL to match the active configuration. Workaround: Reapply the ACL on the primary switch to each interface where it is applied. This triggers proper synchronization to the secondary switch. The following is an example of proper synchronization: vlan 110 apply access-list ip list1 out vlan 120 apply access-list ip list1 out vlan 130 apply access-list ip list1 out.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1020, what issue was resolved in BGP Bug 360748?
original_answer: BGP (Bug ID 360748): Symptom: BGP selects the wrong path, causing routing issues. Scenario: In some scenarios BGP incorrectly selects the path with the highest AS-Path. This causes incorrect paths to be propagated to the Routing Information Base (RIB) and Forwarding Information Base (FIB), potentially causing routing issues. Workaround: Lower the local preference of EVPN originated router. This causes the higher local preference route to be selected.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1020, what issue was resolved in VXLAN Bug 359279?
original_answer: VXLAN (Bug ID 359279): Symptom: After a virtual machine is migrated (vMotion) from one data center to another in a static VXLAN environment, the VM cannot reach its gateway or external destinations, resulting in ping timeouts and connection failures. Scenario: In some scenarios, the switch can fail to program tunnel to VNI associations, leading to traffic loss. Workaround: Remove the affected VNI from the configuration on the switches in the source data center and then add the VNI to the configuration again.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1020, what issue was resolved in DHCP Relay Bug 359666?
original_answer: DHCP Relay (Bug ID 359666): Symptom: With hpe-relay core dump legacy bootp client does not get IP. Scenario: When the DHCP relay daemon processes a legacy BOOTP client packet that lacks the option-53 field, it fails to properly validate the packet's structure before attempting to access this field, leading to a crash. This issue occurs exclusively when the smart-relay feature is enabled. Workaround: Disable the global DHCP smart-relay configuration if not required using no dhcp-smart-relay.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1020, what issue was resolved in Multicast Bug 342612?
original_answer: Multicast (Bug ID 342612): Symptom: When switching between IPTV channels, there is an intermittent delay of 2-6 seconds before the new video stream is displayed to the end user. Scenario: The system experiences a small delay in the program of a new multicast flow after receiving the IGMP report. During this time, critical initial video packets containing SPS (Sequence Parameter Set) and PPS (Picture Parameter Set) information may be dropped. These packets are essential for the decoder to properly display the H.264 video stream. Workaround: Configure static IGMP groups for the multicast streams using static igmp join <multicast-group-address> interface <decoder-port>. This ensures the hardware is pre-programmed with the multicast flows, eliminating the join latency.

original_answer: VSX (Bug ID 355409): Symptom: An egress loop can be seen leading to replicated BUM traffic whenever the LC reboots in a VSX setup. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1020, what issue was resolved in REST Bug 353178?
original_answer: REST (Bug ID 353178): Symptom: When uploading firmware using the Web UI or REST API, the system displays the following error message despite the firmware being successfully transferred to the device: There was an error uploading new firmware. Firmware image is invalid. Please enter a valid firmware image. Scenario: When logging console is enabled in the switch configuration and a user attempts to upload firmware through the Web UI or REST API, the upload process is completed, but an HTTP 500 error is generated. Workaround: Until upgrading to the fixed version, use one of the following methods: 1. Disable console logging by removing logging console from the configuration. Then attempt the firmware upload using the Web UI or REST API again. 2. Use TFTP for firmware upload by uploading the firmware using TFTP or SFTP instead of the Web UI or REST API. Example command: copy tftp/sftp://[server-ip]/[firmware-file] primary vrf mgmt.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1020, what issue was resolved in VLANs Bug 348612?
original_answer: VLANs (Bug ID 348612): Symptom: When wireless clients roam between access points connected to the same switch, they lose connectivity and receive an APIPA address. Connectivity is only restored after the MAC-age timer expires. Scenario: When a client is authenticated with a VLAN on a secure port, the VLAN is added as an ingress-only VLAN on other secure ports, but the MSTP state for those ports remains in blocked state instead of forwarding state, preventing seamless client movement. Workaround: Reduce the MAC-age timer to minimize the impact. or configure AP ports in device-mode instead of client-mode as follows: interface 1/1/X port-access client-limit 1 port-access client-mode device.

original_answer: DHCP (Bug ID 348727): Symptom: DHCP server configuration status becomes stuck in a pending state after configuration changes are pushed from Aruba Central, preventing proper DHCP service operation. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1020, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what issue was resolved in ARP Bug 351751?
original_answer: ARP (Bug ID 351751): Symptom: After a server failover, the new MAC address is not updated on the remote VTEP VSX secondary node. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what issue was resolved in Boot Process Bug 319610?
original_answer: Boot Process (Bug ID 319610): Symptom: When switching between IPTV channels, there is an intermittent delay of 2-6 seconds before the new video stream is displayed to the end user. Scenario: The system experiences a small delay in the program of a new multicast flow after receiving the IGMP report. During this time, critical initial video packets containing SPS (Sequence Parameter Set) and PPS (Picture Parameter Set) information may be dropped. These packets are essential for the decoder to properly display the H.264 video stream. Workaround: Configure static IGMP groups for the multicast streams as follows: static igmp join <multicast-group-address> interface <decoder-port> This ensures the hardware is pre-programmed with the multicast flows, eliminating the join latency. configure interface IP and Active gateway IP/MAC when interface is UP and running.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what issue was resolved in SNMP Bug 367181?
original_answer: SNMP (Bug ID 367181): Symptom: Switches may fail to respond to SNMP queries after upgrading from 10.08 version of code to 10.13 code. Scenario: This issue occurs when a switch has been configured with snmp-server response-source prior to upgrade. Workaround: After upgrading, access the switch shell and either: 1. Delete the file /fs/nos/net-snmp/agent-swns/snmp.conf, or 2. Edit the file to comment out (with #) the line containing [snmp] srcipaddr entry 3. Restart the SNMP service with systemctl restart snmpd-wrapper-in-c.service.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what issue was resolved in RADIUS Bug 363074?
original_answer: RADIUS (Bug ID 363074): Symptom: The switch intermittently fails to include the Framed-IP attribute in RADIUS accounting packets. This results in missing IP address information for authenticated clients. Scenario: The issue is observed when a client disconnects and reconnects after a short interval (for example, 90 seconds). The Framed-IP attribute may be missing even though the client has a valid IP address and is visible in other system outputs. Workaround: Ensure continuous client traffic after reconnection to allow the switch to relearn the IP address. Alternatively, re-enable MAC authentication on the port or perform a shut/no shut cycle to restore expected behavior.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1050, what issue was resolved in EVPN Bug 379727?
original_answer: EVPN (Bug ID 379727): Symptom: There is a resource leak when an L3 host (ARP / ND) moves between local port (non-VxLAN) to VxLAN tunnel. Over a period of time and especially under scaled L3 hosts environments, this slow resource leak may lead the system to run out of L3 resources critical for neighbor and route forwarding, leading to L3 connectivity issues. Scenario: With VxLAN deployments, if a host moves between a local port to VxLAN tunnel, there is a critical resource leak with every host move. When there are a large number of mobile wireless devices changing wireless access point connections, specifically between locally connected (non-VxLAN) and over VxLAN tunnels, L3 resources needed for neighbor / route forwarding may run out. Workaround: Reboot the box to reclaim leaked resources. In a VSX deploymeny, reboot the secondary device, then reboot the primary device after the secondary device comes back up to keep downtime minimal.
cleaned_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1050, for HPE Aruba AOS-CX 10.15.1050, what issue was resolved in the EVPN feature related to Bug ID 379727?
cleaned_answer: EVPN (Bug ID 379727): Symptom: There is a resource leak when an L3 host (ARP / ND) moves between local port (non-VxLAN) and VxLAN tunnel. Over time, especially in scaled environments with many mobile wireless devices changing access points, this slow resource leak can exhaust critical L3 resources needed for neighbor and route forwarding, leading to connectivity issues. Scenario: In VxLAN deployments, if a host moves between a local port to a VxLAN tunnel, there is a critical resource leak with each move. This issue becomes more pronounced in environments with many mobile devices switching access points. Workaround: Reboot the device to reclaim leaked resources. In a VSX deployment, reboot the secondary device first, then the primary after the secondary comes back up to minimize downtime.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what issue was resolved in Logging Bug 358847?
original_answer: Logging (Bug ID 358847): Symptom: A VSF stack member switch unexpectedly reboots due to the ops-switchd process crashing with a signal: 6 error. Scenario: In some scenarios with excessive ACL event logging, a crash may occur due to a failure in the system when processing a high volume of ACL log entries. Workaround: Minimize the use of the log keyword in ACL entries, especially on high-traffic rules. Consider using ACL counters instead of logging for monitoring traffic patterns.

original_answer: L3 routes (Bug ID 365274): Symptom: Network Management Systems does not display IPv4 addresses for Aruba CX switches running AOS-CX version 10.13. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what issue was resolved in SNMP Bug 367181?
original_answer: SNMP (Bug ID 367181): Symptom: Switches may fail to respond to SNMP queries after upgrading from 10.08 version of code to 10.13 code. Scenario: This issue occurs when a switch has been configured with the "snmp-server response-source" command prior to upgrade. Workaround: After upgrading, access the switch shell and either: 1. Delete the file/fs/nos/net-snmp/agent-swns/snmp.conf 2. Edit the file to comment out (with #) the line containing[snmp] srcipaddrentry 3. Restart the SNMP service with systemctl restart snmpd-wrapper-in-c.service.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what issue was resolved in DHCP Relay Bug 371954?
original_answer: DHCP Relay (Bug ID 371954): Symptom: In an L2VPN network, when a DHCP client sends an IP request with the broadcast flag unset (unicast), duplicate DHCP client packets are observed at the DHCP server-one from each VTEP. As a result, the client does not receive an IP address. Scenario: To prevent duplicate DHCP client packets from each VTEP in an L2VPN topology, it is recommended to configure "no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients" on the switch. However, after saving the switch configuration and rebooting, the relay daemon does not correctly apply all configuration settings at boot time, even though the configuration persists in the database. This causes the duplicate packet issue to reappear. Workaround: To restore correct behavior, manually enable and then disable the L2VPN client configuration using "dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients" followed by "no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients".

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.16.1020, what issue was resolved in SNMP Bug 400261?
original_answer: SNMP (Bug ID 400261): Symptom: SNMP OID sysUpTimeInstance (1.3.6.1.2.1.1.3.0) may return an incorrect value when polled, leading to inaccurate uptime reporting in monitoring systems. Scenario: This issue occurs on switches running AOS-CX 10.16 when using SNMP to query sysUpTimeInstance. The reported value does not match the actual system uptime, causing discrepancies in monitoring tools. Workaround: Use the OID hrSystemUptime (1.3.6.1.2.1.25.1.1.0) instead of sysUpTimeInstance for accurate system uptime reporting.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.16.1030, what issue was resolved in SNMP Bug 408309?
original_answer: SNMP (Bug ID 408309): Symptom: SNMPv3 user password changes do not take effect, after deleting and re-creating the user with a new password, the device still accepts the old password and rejects the new one. Scenario: This issue may occur when deleting an existing SNMPv3 user and re-adding the same username with a different 'auth-pass'; authentication succeeds only with the original password. Workaround: Create a new SNMPv3 user with the desired password and do not reuse the original username.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what issue was resolved in MAC Tables Bug 415095?
original_answer: MAC Tables (Bug ID 415095): Symptom: MACs are only showing on one side of a VSX pair. For instance, a MAC is learned on a physical port on one VSX device and is missing from the peer VSX device. Scenario: This issue can occur after resolving a network loop which causes the MACs to quickly move from one VSX device to the other, particularly when MACs are moving from EVPN tunnels to physical ports. Workaround: Using the CLI to clear the problematic MAC address.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what issue was resolved in VSX Bug 416422?
original_answer: VSX (Bug ID 416422): Symptom: CPU bound traffic (like an ICMP ping) to the peer that has active-gateway kernel programming issues fails. hpe-vsxd logs errors surrounding active-gateway programming for a given SVI for each VSX event in the future once failed. Scenario: This issue may occur when migrating SVIs with virtual active-gateway configuration to a new VRF. If kernel programming fails then all future VSX related events also retry the programming and fail again (no corrective action). Workaround: Saving config and rebooting the affected VSX peer resolves the kernel programming issue.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what issue was resolved in CPU Rx Bug 411613?
original_answer: CPU Rx (Bug ID 411613): Symptom: Several protocols, such as ROP port, LLDP, and LACP, do not work. Scenario: This issue may occur if no module configuration is issued to any LC and the LC is restored or a different LC with module is configured, the previous configuration applied to the system does not recover, even though the same configuration was working prior to no module. Workaround: Reboot the switch. If reboot is not possible, and LC needs be swapped, remove any configuration on the LC before performing no module.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what known issue is documented for PBR Bug 407418?
original_answer: PBR (Bug ID 407418): Symptom: Classifier Policy is not enforced for traffic coming from an ISL link. Scenario: For a topology with VSX+ VRRP, if the policy is applied on SVI, all packets which reach the VRRP secondary device go to the primary device via the ISL. All of those packets are not getting classifier policy enforced. Even though the policy action is PBR redirect, those packets are not getting redirected. Workaround: Two workarounds are possible to prevent packets from being forwarded to VSX primary via ISL. 1. Use active gateway to give better active active redundancy. Packets reaching either VSX device process the packet destined to active gateway. 2. Turn off the interface connection from VRRP secondary device to down switch. This method may cause redundancy and is not preferred.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what issue was resolved in VXLAN Bug 379727?
original_answer: VXLAN (Bug ID 379727): Symptom: There is a resource leak when an L3 host (ARP / ND) moves between local port (non-VxLAN) to VXLAN tunnel. Over period of time and especially under scaled L3 hosts environments, this slow resource leak, may lead system to run out of L3 resources critical for neighbor and route forwarding, leading to L3 connectivity issues. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.17.1001, what issue was resolved in Central connectivity Bug 392076?
original_answer: Central connectivity (Bug ID 392076): Symptom: Switch is unable to connect to Central. Scenario: This issue may occur when attempting to onboard switches that are connected to Central. The process connecting to YANG is creating more sockets than expected, leading to overload of the yang-resolverd server and subsequent disconnections. Workaround: Restart yang-resolverd daemon to reestablish the operation of the daemon. Restart hpe-restd daemon to make a clean connection to YANG after restarting the yang-resolverd daemon.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.17.1001, what issue was resolved in SNMP Bug 408309?
original_answer: SNMP (Bug ID 408309): Symptom: SNMPv3 user password changes do not take effect, after deleting and re-creating the user with a new password, the device still accepts the old password and rejects the new one. Scenario: This issue occurs after deleting an existing SNMPv3 user and re-add the same username with a different 'auth-pass'; authentication succeeds only with the original password. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.17.1001, what issue was resolved in Classifier Bug 401837?
original_answer: Classifier (Bug ID 401837): Symptom: After upgrading to 10.17.0001, LAG member interfaces remain stuck in “Initializing” state. Scenario: This issue occurs during upgrade or reboot on 10.17.0001 when a LAG contains one or more member ports on a line card that is down. Workaround: Before upgrade/reboot, remove from the LAG any member ports that belong to the down line card (or bring that line card online). If already impacted, remove the down-LC member ports from the LAG; the remaining members come up and the LAG forms.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what caveat is documented for BGP?

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what issue was resolved in MAC Tables Bug 415095?
original_answer: MAC Tables (Bug ID 415095): Symptom: MACs are only showing on one side of a VSX pair. For instance, a MAC is learned on a physical port on one VSX device and is missing from the peer VSX device. Workaround: Using the CLI to clear the problematic MAC address.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what issue was resolved in VSX Bug 416422?
original_answer: VSX (Bug ID 416422): Symptom: CPU bound traffic (like an ICMP ping) to the peer that has active-gateway kernel programming issues fails. hpe-vsxd logs errors surrounding active-gateway programming for a given SVI for each VSX event in the future once failed. Scenario: This issue may occur when migrating SVIs with virtual active-gateway configuration to a new VRF. If kernel programming fails then all future VSX related events also retry the programming and fail again (no corrective action). Workaround: Saving config and rebooting the affected VSX peer resolves the kernel programming issue.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what known issue is documented for CPU Rx Bug 411613?
original_answer: CPU Rx (Bug ID 411613): Symptom: Several protocols, such as ROP port, LLDP, and LACP, do not work. Scenario: This issue may occur if no module configuration is issued to any LC and the LC is restored or a different LC with module is configured, the previous configuration applied to the system does not recover, even though the same configuration was working prior to no module. Workaround: Reboot the switch. If reboot is not possible, and LC needs be swapped, remove any configuration on the LC before performing no module.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what known issue is documented for PBR Bug 407418?
original_answer: PBR (Bug ID 407418): Symptom: Classifier Policy is not enforced for traffic coming from an ISL link. Scenario: For a topology with VSX+ VRRP, if the policy is applied on SVI, all packets which reach the VRRP secondary device go to the primary device via the ISL. All of those packets are not getting classifier policy enforced. Even though the policy action is PBR redirect, those packets are not getting redirected. Workaround: Two workarounds are possible to prevent packets from being forwarded to VSX primary via ISL. 1. Use active gateway to give better active active redundancy. Packets reaching either VSX device process the packet destined to active gateway. 2. Turn off the interface connection from VRRP secondary device to down switch. This method may cause redundancy and is not preferred.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.18.0001, what issue was resolved in DHCP Relay Bug 423594?
original_answer: DHCP Relay (Bug ID 423594): Symptom: DHCP clients connected to a switch configured as a DHCP relay agent may fail to obtain an IP address when DHCPv4 Snooping and DHCPv4 Relay are both enabled on the same VLAN. DHCP clients that perform explicit source MAC address validation may drop the relayed DHCP response packets. Scenario: This issue occurs when both DHCPv4 Relay and DHCPv4 Snooping are enabled on the same VLAN. In this configuration, DHCP server responses (Offer/Ack) are forwarded to the client with an invalid (all-zeros) source MAC address in the Ethernet header. Workaround: Avoid enabling DHCPv4 Snooping and DHCPv4 Relay simultaneously on the same VLAN.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.18.0001, what issue was resolved in PKI Bug 428981?
original_answer: PKI (Bug ID 428981): Symptom: EST-based certificate auto-enrollment and re-enrollment fails with an SSL connect error (CURL error 35) on AOS-CX 10.16 and later when the EST server requires TLS client certificate authentication and enforces it via TLS renegotiation. Scenario: This issue may occur when the EST client first retrieves CA certificates over an unauthenticated TLS connection and caches the session. When the server subsequently requests a client certificate by initiating TLS renegotiation on the reused session, the switch rejects the renegotiation, causing enrollment to fail. This behavior is observed with EST servers such as EJBCA when configured to require mutual TLS (mTLS) client certificate authentication. Workaround: Configure the EST server to accept password-based authentication (HTTP Digest or Basic) as an alternative to requiring TLS client certificate authentication on the enrollment endpoint, and configure username and password credentials in the AOS-CX EST profile. This changes the authentication method for EST enrollment.

This changes the authentication method for EST enrollment.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.18.0001, what issue was resolved in UDP Forwarder Bug 418074?
original_answer: UDP Forwarder (Bug ID 418074): Symptom: When ip forward-protocol udp is configured with a non-classful subnet broadcast address as the destination (for example, 10.232.104.127 for a /25 subnet), the forwarded packet is sent as unicast to that address instead of being broadcast on the destination subnet. Hosts on the destination subnet do not receive the forwarded UDP broadcast. Scenario: This issue occurs when the destination address configured in ip forward-protocol udp is a non-classful subnet broadcast address (not ending in.255). The UDP broadcast forwarder only recognizes classful subnet broadcast addresses (ending in.255) as broadcast destinations. Any other address, including valid subnet broadcast addresses for prefix lengths shorter than /24 (such as /25, /26, etc.), is treated as a unicast destination. The switch identifies a configured address as a broadcast destination solely based on whether the last octet is 255, regardless of the subnet prefix length. Workaround: Use only classful subnet broadcast addresses (ending in.255) as destinations in ip forward-protocol udp when broadcast delivery on the destination subnet is required. original_answer: BGP (Bug ID 410572): Symptom: BGP peer group member sessions do not reset when the include-TCP-options setting is modified, while individual peer sessions reset as expected. Scenario: This issue occurs when modifying or removing the include-TCP-options setting on a BGP peer group that has TCP Authentication Option (TCP-AO) configured. Individual peer sessions respond correctly to the configuration change by resetting, but peer group member sessions remain established without resetting.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.18.0001, what issue was resolved in BGP Bug 404735?
original_answer: BGP (Bug ID 404735): Symptom: BGP session with TCP Authentication Option (TCP-AO) remains in Established state even when a keychain key mismatch exists between peers. The session does not reset when the matching key is deleted from one peer, and manual session reset using clear bgp \* does not resolve the issue. This creates a security vulnerability as the session continues operating with mismatched authentication parameters. Scenario: This issue occurs when a matching keychain key is deleted from one BGP peer after both peers have been configured with the same key. The deletion creates a parameter mismatch between the two peers (different send-id, recv-id, or key string values), but the BGP session fails to detect this mismatch and remains established instead of resetting.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.18.0001, what issue was resolved in BGP Bug 359034?
original_answer: BGP (Bug ID 359034): Symptom: Attempts to unconfigure a keychain that is currently being used by a routing protocol (BGP with TCP-AO), result in the system displaying a confirmation message warning that the action disrupts protocol adjacencies. original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.18.0001, what issue was resolved in BGP Bug 414430?
original_answer: BGP (Bug ID 414430): Symptom: When BGP graceful restart is triggered on a TCP-AO authenticated BGP peer, the peer router does not mark the received routes as stale (indicated by the S prefix). Instead, routes are immediately removed from the routing table, causing a temporary loss of traffic visibility and convergence delay until the restarting peer comes back online. Scenario: This issue occurs when performing a graceful restart (redundancy switchover or hpe-routing restart) on a BGP peer that has TCP Authentication Option (TCP-AO) configured with keychain authentication. The peer router fails to enter graceful restart helper mode and does not preserve routes with stale markers during the restart window.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.18.0001, what known issue is documented for VSX Bug 425034?
original_answer: VSX (Bug ID 425034): Symptom: ISL connectivity may be affected as the full MTU override has not been applied to some ports. VSX connectivity between peers may be affected. Scenario: This issue may occur during bring up of ISL ports and may result in MTU override not being set correctly during port configuration. Ports that were missed during this configuration remain missed, even if more ports are added to ISL later. Workaround: Fully unconfigure the ISL and affected ports then reconfigure for proper MTU override to be applied.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.18.0001, what known issue is documented for DHCP Server Bug 420285?
original_answer: DHCP Server (Bug ID 420285): Symptom: DHCP clients connected via a relay agent either fail to obtain an IP address or receive an IP address from an incorrect DHCP pool instead of the pool intended for their subnet. Scenario: This issue occurs when the DHCP server has multiple pools configured with IP ranges that span non-default subnet boundaries (e.g., /23), and the prefix-len parameter is not explicitly specified in the range command. When a relay agent forwards client DISCOVER packets, the DHCP server uses the relay agent's gateway IP address (giaddr) to identify the matching pool. Without prefix-len, the server cannot determine the pool's subnet boundary and fails to correctly map the giaddr to the intended pool. Workaround: Explicitly specify prefix-len in the pool range configuration to match the intended subnet mask. For example: dhcp-server vrf default pool pool_name_ABC range 172.22.0.4 172.22.1.254 prefix-len <netmask>.

original_question: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.18.0001, what known issue is documented for BGP Bug 341596?
original_answer: BGP (Bug ID 341596): Symptom: Routes take up to two minutes to switch to an alternate path when an active BGP neighbor is disabled, leading to delays in traffic rerouting. Scenario: This issue occurs in a network utilizing BGP routing. When a BGP session is disabled on one switch, the corresponding routes remain in a stale state until the stale path timer expires, causing traffic loss during the transition.
